world.title: New Security Council Draft on Hormuz Eases Off Threats of Force
world.description: Bahrain on Monday circulated a draft Security Council resolution on reopening the Strait of Hormuz that removes language around the possible use of force, as supporters of the document tried to head off possible vetoes by Russia or China.

world.description: Iran's move comes as Trump has set a Tuesday, 8 pm Eastern Time deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key global shipping route.

world.description: SABC News The Beitbridge Border Post in Musina is expecting traffic congestion from Sunday night as holidaymakers return to the country after the Easter weekend. The port of entry has been seeing a balanced flow of traffic , with a minimum number of buses and trucks passing by. world.description: The Bryansk plant "Kremniy El," after being hit by the Ukrainian Defense Forces on March 10, is operating in emergency recovery mode – production at the enterprise has been halted. — Ukrinform.

world.title: UN’s top oost and Ndayishimiye’s scandalous AU ambush
world.description: By Paul Ejime WHEN Burundi's tenth President, Évariste Ndayishimiye, 57, was elected Chairperson of the African Union (AU) last February, many political commentators paid little attention. It was only for one year and a routine rotational position of limited significance. The former military rebel leader is now using the position for self-aggrandizement, rubbishing his country, which gained political independence

business.title: Phillip Research initiates ‘buy’ call on CBH, expects share price to nearly double
business.description: KUALA LUMPUR (April 7): Phillip Research has initiated a 'buy' call on CBH Engineering Holdings Bhd (KL:CBHB) with a target price of 91 sen, as the house sees the group as a key beneficiary of Malaysia’s accelerating power infrastructure investment cycle driven by the rapid expansion of data centres.

science.title: Artemis II moon crew flies farther than humans have ever gone before
science.description: HOUSTON: The four astronauts of NASA’s Artemis II mission flew deeper into space on Monday than any humans before them, as they ​cruised through a rare flyby of the shadowed far side of the moon that revealed a lunar surface under cosmic bombardment. science.title: Not Science Fiction: Potatoes can grow in lunar soil if compost is added
science.description: Picture it: You’ve traveled three days — nearly 250,000 miles. You step off the space shuttle onto the powdery surface of the moon. You think to yourself, 'Man, fries would hit hard right about now.'

science.title: New strategy exploits specific genetic vulnerability in colorectal cancer patients
science.description: Researchers have identified a novel therapeutic approach for colorectal cancer by exploiting a specific genetic vulnerability found in a majority of patients.

science.title: New method rapidly analyzes cell proteins and metabolites
science.description: Researchers at Cedars-Sinai have developed a fast, new technique for analyzing cells, described in the journal Angewandte Chemie. The approach, called single-injection multi-omics analysis by direct infusion (SMAD), can detect more than 1,300 proteins and more than 9,000 molecular features from a single sample in less than five minutes.

science.title: Artemis II moon crew breaks Cold War-era spaceflight distance record
science.description: HOUSTON, April 6 (Reuters) - The four astronauts of NASA's Artemis II mission flew deeper into space on Monday than any humans before them, as they cruised through a rare flyby of the shadowed far side of the moon that revealed a lunar surface under cosmic bombardment.

ai.title: AI-generated artists break through in country music
